assertion_results.text: Output must address ownership ambiguity explicitly
assertion_results.evidence: Executive summary: 'No incident tracking, no product owner, 3 VPs in 18 months — structural instability is severe'. Medium term: 'Assign an interim product owner or a rotating engineering lead to represent product priorities'.

assertion_results.text: Output must mention both CI systems by name (CircleCI and GitHub Actions)
assertion_results.evidence: Short term: 'Designate one CI system (GitHub Actions recommended) as the single authoritative pipeline; disable or archive CircleCI for deployment triggers.' Both named explicitly.

assertion_results.text: Output must give at least one short-term action item that is actionable despite the chaos
assertion_results.evidence: 'Designate GitHub Actions as sole authoritative CI; decommission CircleCI deploy triggers' and 'Run the full test suite immediately; triage results into passing, failing, and flaky buckets.' Both are concrete actions a 7-person team can execute independently.

assertion_results.text: Output must score Ownership & culture at 2/5 or lower
assertion_results.evidence: Ownership & culture | 2/5 | No product owner, leadership churn, shared accountability absent.

assertion_results.text: Output must not recommend a full team reorganisation
assertion_results.evidence: Long term: 'Stabilize organizational context: advocate for a consistent engineering leadership tenure' — advocacy only, not a directive to reorganise. No structural team changes recommended anywhere in the plan.
